rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MARK JONES' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiffs have alleged sufficient facts to show a unity of interest and ownership, and an unjust result if the alter ego liability were not imposed. rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA GRALLA VS. TARA EWING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By Catholic Healthcare West Dba Saint Francis
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 3, Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By Saint Francis Medical Center and Catholic Healthcare West (part 1 of 2). Defendants Saint Francis Memorial Hospital and Catholic Healthcare West's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Plaintiff's objections to Defendants' expert declarations are overruled. Defendants' objections to Plaintiff's expert declarations are sustained. The Curriculum Vitaes attached as exhibits to the declarations reveal that neither expert has practiced in a psychiatric care facility for adults in at least twenty years. Therefore, their opinions on the current psychiatric care standards lack foundation. (Ev. Code sec. 403.) As to the first cause of action for willful misconduct, the motion is granted. Defendants met their burden by submitting evidence showing that they acted within the professional standard of care. (See Declaration of Rona Hu, MD, 4-6; Declaration of J. Simmy Palecko, MSN, RN, CNS, 4, 8.) The burden then shifted to Plaintiff to provide evidence showing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants acted with intent to harm or reckless indifference to known consequences. (Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 412.) Plaintiff has not met her burden, as the evidence submitted shows, at most, negligence, which is insufficient to support a willful misconduct cause of action. As to the second cause of action for elder/dependant adult abuse, the motion is granted. Defendants met their burden by submitting evidence showing that they acted within the professional standard of care. (See Declaration of Rona Hu, MD, 4-6; Declaration of J. Simmy Palecko, MSN, RN, CNS, 4, 8.) The burden then shifted to Plaintiff to provide evidence showing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants engaged in "conduct essentially equivalent to conduct that would support recovery of punitive damages." (Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 778-89.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of producing evidence that shows that Defendants acted with the intent to harm or reckless indifference. As to the third cause of action for premises liability, the motion is denied. Defendants met their burden of showing that Defendants did not breach their duty to Plaintiff because Defendant acted appropriately and within the requisite standard of care under the circumstances. (See Declaration of Rona Hu, MD, 4-6; Declaration of J. Simmy Palecko, MSN, RN, CNS, 4, 8.) (TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED; SEE PART 2) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA GRALLA VS. TARA EWING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Saint Francis Memorial Hospital And Catholic Healthcare West'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of 2).
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 3, Defendants Saint Francis Memorial Hospital And Catholic Healthcare West'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of 2). The burden then shifted to Plaintiff to provide evidence show a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants breached their duty. Plaintiffs set forth evidence showing that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants acted with negligence by not monitoring Tara Ewing one-on-one following the attack on Plaintiff and whether Defendants complied with internal procedures put in place to ensure the safety of patients on the premises after a violent occurrence. (Knops Decl., Ex. D, p. 175, Ex. G, p. 014.) As to the fourth cause of action for violation of confidentiality of medical records and right to privacy, the motion is denied. Defendants have not met their burden. The only evidence set forth shows that Defendants got Plaintiff's consent prior to releasing records to defense counsel, but there is no evidence submitted showing Defendants had Plaintiff's consent to release her records to the District Attorney. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ST. LUCIA FREE PRESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of First Amended Petition To Quash Invalid Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 4, PETITIONER ST. LUCIA FREE PRESS' Petition To Quash Invalid Subpoena. Granted. The subpoena duces tecum issued and served on Automattic, Inc is quashed. Further, Oliver Gobat shall not issue or cause to be issued any further subpoenas to Automattic, Inc., seeking the identity or identifying information regarding the poster of the allegedly defamatory speech that is the subject of this petition. (See Motion, Exhibit H; Opp., Gobat Decl., Exhibits, B, C.). The Court applies the balancing test endorsed by Krinsky v. DOE 6 (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 1154, 1165. The Court finds that Mr. Gobat has not stated a prima facie case of defamation; the anonymous comments complained of are opinion mixed with sarcasm and hyperbole, rather than objective statements of fact. (See Krinsky v. DOE 6, supra, 159 Cal.App.4th at 1175-1178.) The Court is bound to follow the First Amendment of the United States in determining the liability of defamation First Amendment of the U.S. Constitution trumps English law in deciding whether a prima facie case for defamation has been made. (Id. at 1173.) The Court need not and does not determine whether Mr. Gobat is a public figure. Monetary sanctions of $15,000 are awarded in favor of St Lucia Free Press and against Oliver Gobat to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order . The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOEY HARP et al VS. MICHELLE MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Comep Answers To Form Interrogatories, Demand For Production; Mtn To Have Req.'S For Admissions Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MICHELLE MILLER's Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Demand For Production; Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Michelle Miller's motion to compel answers to Defendant's Set Two Form Interrogatories is DENIED. The interrogatories were previously included in Defendant's Set One Form Interrogatories that are the subject of another motion that was granted today. Defendant's motion to compel responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories - Set One and for an order deeming responses to Defendant's Requests for Admissions - Set Two, is GRANTED. Within 20 days from service of notice of entry of this order, plaintiffs Joey Harp and Moriah Nangle shall each, independently: (1) serve Defendant Michelle Miller with code-compliant verified responses, without objection, to Defendant's Special Interrogatories - Set One. It is further ordered that Plaintiff Joey Harp and Plaintiff Moriah Nangle are both DEEMED to have Admitted each and every Request for Admission in Defendant's Requests for Admissions, Set Two that were attached to Defendant's Motion. Plaintiffs Joe Harp and Moriah Nangle shall collectively pay Plaintiff Michelle Miller sanctions totaling $360.00 within 30 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ' Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pursuant to Cannon 6D(5) of the California Code of Judicial Conduct, Pro Tem Judge Halperin discloses that he was a law partner in a firm in private practice with a cousin of Plaintiff's counsel Lawrence Scancarelli until December of 2007. He does not believe this fact requires recusal under California Code of Judicial Ethics Cannon 6(D)(3), but encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure prior to the hearing. Plaintiff's motion for an order establishing admissions and for sanctions is GRANTED. In response to a motion brought under C.C.P. Section 2033-280(b) to deem admitted the truth of matters specified in requests for admissions, a court shall make such an order unless it finds that the party to whom the requests for admission have been directed has served, before the hearing on the motion, a proposed response to the requests for admissions that is in substantial compliance with CCP Section 2033.220. Defendant Anthony Novak has provided no evidence of any such response to Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions - Set One. The truth of the matters specified in Plaintiff's requests for admissions numbers 1 through 8 are DEEMED ADMITTED. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,075.00. Defendant Anthony Novak shall pay $1,075.00 to Plaintiff Elvia Juarez within 30 days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RODOLFO CASTRO et al VS. WILLIAM C. LYNN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 21, DEFENDANT WILLIAM C. LYNN, TRUSTEE OF THE WILLIAM C. LYNN TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to the 8th cause of action to foreclose on the mechanic's lien as unopposed. The Court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to the breach of contract causes of action. A violation of Business & Professions Code sec. 17918 is a curable defect and the proposed 2nd Amended Complaint now alleges compliance. A factual issue remains regarding whether registering Calisco rather than Calisco Builders & Painting was proper and allows plaintiffs to proceed. The proposed 2nd AC also alleges that plaintiff Castro was duly licensed during all relevant times. Whether that allegation is in fact true raises a factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Business & Professions Code sec. 7159 is not a basis to bar plaintiff's claims. The proposed amended complaint alleges that the contracts were in writing. In addition, courts may enforce oral home improvement contracts in certain instances. See Hinerfeld-Ward v. Lipian (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 86. Lastly, plaintiff has leave to amplify the agency allegations and plead any other applicable claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HO'S CONSTRUCTION INC. VS. JOSHUA SKENES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF HO'S CONSTRUCTION INC.'s MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. rulings.Case Title: JOHN TERENZIO et al VS. CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTs CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION and CURRENT MEDIA, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. (Continued to part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN TERENZIO et al VS. CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Current Tv, Llc And Current Media, Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTs CURRENT TV, LLC and CURRENT MEDIA, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents (Part 2 of 2). The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Defendants' requests for documents reflecting the terms of Plaintiff Terenzio's employment related to the Judge Joe Brown, Swift Justice, Fox Cable Networks CCTV engagement, HRTV, and the Fox dating show seek information relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action. Plaintiff's complaint alleges that "Terenzio's presentation to Blum was based on the mutual understanding that Terenzio would be compensated both as a finder and for his media-related services, if Current consummated its sale to Al Jazeera" (Complaint Par. 22) and that, "Terenzio proposed that in addition to his services as a finder, he would consult and manage the public relations and creative aspects of the sale (Complaint Par. 23)." Moreover, Plaintiff testified about the terms of the contracts at his deposition. Under these circumstances, the state's interest in facilitating the ascertainment of truth in connection with legal proceedings is substantial enough to compel disclosure of these particular contracts, but not documents reflecting other employment engagements dating back to 1991 that Defendants also seek. Plaintiff shall produce copies of any executed contracts related to the Judge Joe Brown, Swift Justice, CCTV, HRTV, and Fox dating show engagements, and may designate the contracts as "CONFIDENTIAL - OUTSIDE COUNSEL'S EYES ONLY" pursuant to a stipulated protective order. The parties shall, prior to the hearing, meet and confer and then at the hearing present the court with a stipulated protective order. The proposed order must contain provisions complying with the requirements of California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551. The parties can find model stipulated protective orders that meet these requirements on the Complex Litigation Page of the San Mateo County Superior Court website and on the Los Angeles Superior Court's Complex Court Informational Materials website page. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA COEN VS. GENERAL NUTRITION CENTERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTs GENERAL NUTRITION CENTERS, INC. and DARIN VILHAUER's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted. The prayer for punitive damages as to Defendant GNC is stricken without leave to amend. Specifically, the following language is stricken from the First Amended Complaint as to Defendant GNC: p. 6, lines 5 - 12; p. 8, lines 8 - 16; p. 9, lines 13 - 21. Plaintiff has not pled the requisite facts to assert a punitive damages claim against Defendant GNC, a corporate defendant. (Civ. Code § 3294(b).) The motion is granted without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has already had an opportunity to remedy this defect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHUN YUN VS. JOE'S UNION L.P., DBA ORIGINAL JOE'S et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Chun Yun'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CHUN YUN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories (Part 2 of 2). The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant Joe's Union L.P. misclassified her as exempt and failed to provide rest breaks, meal breaks, and overtime pay during Plaintiff's employment as a pastry chef from January 2012 to September 2012. Plaintiff has moved to compel further responses to Plaintiff's special interrogatories, form interrogatories - General and Employment, Requests for Admissions, and Requests for Production - set one. The court rules as follows: Requests for Admissions: DENIED. Defendant has answered "Deny" to all of Plaintiff's requests for admissions in Plaintiff's Set One RFAs. Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One. GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Nos. 200.1, 200.4, 207.1, 209.2 and 214.1 are not well taken and the responses are incomplete. Special Interrogatories - General, Set One: GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Nos. 3.2, 3.6, 3.7, 4.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 15.1, and 17.1 are not well taken and the responses are incomplete. In responding, Defendant must state the address and telephone summer of each witness in order to provide sufficient information for Plaintiff to subpoena them for trial. Special Interrogatories - Set One: GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Nos. 4, 6-9, 11-12, 15-16, 18-19, 23, 25-26, 28-34 are not well taken, and the response are incomplete. Request for Production of Documents: GRANTED. Defendant has stated that it will produce only Plaintiff's personnel file and payroll records. They contain information that is partially responsive to some of the requests but Plaintiff's requests seek information relevant to the subject matter in the pending action that is likely to be found in other documents as well. Within 30 days Defendant shall serve Plaintiff with code-compliant verified further responses to Form Interrogatories, Requests for Production, and Special Interrogatories (General and Employment), and produce all responsive documents. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INSTITUTO LABORAL DE LA RAZA VS. CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC, DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's entire Complaint is overruled pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e) because the Plaintiff has proper standing under C.C.P. § 369(a). Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's Third and Fourth Causes of Action is overruled pursuant to § 430.10(e) because the Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to constitute these respective causes of action.
